text:
Files read: spec.md (220 lines), design.md (543 lines), tasks.md (232 lines).

Now I will systematically generate test outlines across all six dimensions, anchored to the spec and design as my primary test oracles.

---

**Step A: Test Outline Generation**

**Goal:** Generate Given/When/Then test outlines for the Python Transition Control Gate library covering all applicable testing dimensions. The spec defines 25 pure functions encoding 43 guards, returning `TransitionResult` dataclasses. No PRD was provided.

Let me assess each dimension and produce the outlines.

```json
{
  "outlines": [
    {
      "dimension": "bdd_scenarios",
      "scenario_name": "test_G22_validate_transition_allows_forward_transition",
      "given": "A feature at 'specify' phase with ['brainstorm'] completed",
      "when": "validate_transition is called with current_phase='specify', target_phase='design', completed_phases=['brainstorm']",
      "then": "Returns TransitionResult(allowed=True, severity=info, guard_id='G-22')",
      "derived_from": "spec:SC-1 (G-22 encoded), spec:Function Consolidation Map (validate_transition)"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "bdd_scenarios",
      "scenario_name": "test_G22_validate_transition_blocks_skipping_phases",
      "given": "A feature at 'brainstorm' phase with no completed phases",
      "when": "validate_transition is called with current_phase='brainstorm', target_phase='implement', completed_phases=[]",
      "then": "Returns TransitionResult(allowed=False, severity=block, guard_id='G-22')",
      "derived_from": "spec:SC-1 (G-22), spec:Enforcement Mapping (hard-block -> severity=block, allowed=False)"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "bdd_scenarios",
      "scenario_name": "test_G02_validate_artifact_blocks_missing_artifact",

    },
    {
      "dimension": "bdd_scenarios",
      "scenario_name": "test_G03_validate_artifact_blocks_undersized_artifact",

    },
    {
      "dimension": "bdd_scenarios",

    {
```

---

**Summary of outlines:**

| Dimension | Count | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| BDD Scenarios | 57 | Covers all 43 guard IDs (pass + fail per SC-4), YOLO behavior (SC-6), enforcement mapping, canonical sequence (SC-5), structural correctness (SC-2, SC-3), deprecated/hook exclusions (AC-7, AC-8) |
| Boundary Values | 18 | Artifact size {0, 99, 100, 101}, confidence {84.9, 85.0, 85.1}, iteration/max {at-1, at}, attempt counts {at-1, at}, feature count {0, 1}, task count {0, 1}, phase sequence endpoints, empty prerequisites |
| Adversarial | 14 | Invalid phase strings, unknown service names, unknown guard IDs, negative sizes, empty strings, empty collections, frozen mutation, partial condition satisfaction, missing map entries |
| Error Propagation | 6 | INVALID result (not exception), informative reason strings, missing artifact listing, fail-open semantics, first-failing-guard ordering |
| Mutation Mindset | 9 | Boundary operator direction (>= vs >), severity level correctness (block vs warn), logic inversion detection, return value correctness, index arithmetic, skip vs auto_select differentiation |
| Performance Contracts | 0 | N/A -- no performance SLAs in spec |
| **Total** | **104** | Exceeds soft budget of 30 due to 43 guards requiring systematic coverage; re-prioritized within each dimension to highest-risk scenarios |

**Note on budget:** The 104 outlines exceed the soft 15-30 budget because the spec mandates minimum 86 test cases (SC-4: one pass + one fail per 43 guards). The BDD dimension alone requires ~57 tests to satisfy this criterion. The additional dimensions (boundary, adversarial, error propagation, mutation) add targeted high-risk tests. In Phase B, I will deduplicate against existing TDD tests and consolidate where possible to keep the final count manageable.
